What is Llama and who made it?

Llama (Large Language Model Meta AI) is a family of open-weight large language models developed by Meta AI.

Key facts

  • First released in February 2023
  • Available in multiple sizes (7B, 13B, 70B parameters)
  • Open-weight: weights are publicly available for research
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained("meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf")

How does it compare to GPT-4?

Llama models are generally smaller and more efficient than GPT-4, but GPT-4 leads on most benchmarks requiring complex reasoning or multi-step tasks.

The main trade-off: Llama is open-weight (run locally, fine-tune freely), while GPT-4 is closed (API-only, no fine-tuning of base weights).
